about-intro:
I'm Suzanna. I'm a full-stack developer from Belgium who enjoys building web applications from start to finish. I like working on both the frontend and the backend because I enjoy understanding how everything works together.

I'm always curious to learn something new and I genuinely enjoy improving my projects, whether that's making the code cleaner, the design better or the experience smoother.

When I'm not coding I'm usually making music. I've been writing songs for years and it's become another creative outlet that balances the more technical side of what I do.

I enjoy taking the time to refine the small details because I believe they're what turn a good project into a great user experience.
backend-description: I build clean and reliable APIs and backend systems that are easy to maintain and designed to grow over time.
eurconverted-description:
EURConverted is a web application that makes converting currencies quick and straightforward. The application provides up-to-date exchange rates and allows users to convert between different currencies through a clean and easy-to-use interface.

The application retrieves live exchange rates from an external API and performs conversions instantly. To improve the user experience, recently used currencies and exchange rates are stored locally so the application remains useful even when the internet connection is temporarily unavailable.

During development, the focus was on performance, simplicity and accessibility. The interface was designed to make currency conversion as effortless as possible while providing all the information users need at a glance.

EURConverted was built as a Progressive Web App, allowing it to be installed on mobile devices and used like a native application. This project gave me the opportunity to work with external APIs, offline functionality and modern web technologies while creating a practical tool that can be used every day.
exit-exe-description:
Exit.exe is a single-player escape room game where players solve puzzles, discover clues and uncover the story as they progress through different challenges. The application combines interactive gameplay with an immersive narrative to create an engaging puzzle-solving experience.

The project was developed in a team of four developers. Each team member took ownership of different parts of the application while working closely together to deliver a consistent user experience and a cohesive final product.

The application includes user authentication, multilingual support, game progress tracking and a variety of interactive puzzles that guide players through the story. An AI-powered hint system was also part of the project, although that feature was developed by another team member.

My main contribution focused on frontend development, where I built and refined the user interface and implemented core application features. I also contributed to the application's architecture by helping design a maintainable project structure and collaborating on technical decisions throughout development. This project gave me valuable experience building a modern React application while working closely with other developers in a structured team environment.
frontend-description: I care about creating interfaces that not only look good, but also feel intuitive and enjoyable to use.
full-stack-description: I enjoy working across the entire stack bringing together design, frontend and backend into one cohesive product.
hero-title: Built by curiosity, refined with care.
icedash-description:
IceDash is a mobile endless runner inspired by the classic Chrome dinosaur game, reimagined in a winter setting where players control a penguin that must avoid obstacles while the game gradually becomes faster and more challenging.

Instead of relying on an external game engine, the game was built entirely in React Native. Core mechanics such as gravity, jumping, collision detection, obstacle movement and score progression were implemented from scratch, providing a deeper understanding of game logic and animation within a mobile application.

To create an engaging experience, I focused on visual polish and smooth gameplay. The game features animated sprites, a dynamic snowfall effect, increasing difficulty over time and optional background music. Every element was designed to support the gameplay without distracting the player, resulting in a simple but polished mobile game.

IceDash allowed me to explore game development from a different perspective while strengthening my React Native skills. The project demonstrates my ability to build interactive experiences, solve technical challenges and create smooth animations without relying on dedicated game development frameworks.
kompas-description:
WPL Kompas is a web platform developed for VIVES University of Applied Sciences to support the management of workplace learning. The platform allows administrators, teachers, mentors and students to manage internships, user accounts, evaluations and related administrative tasks through a single application.

This project was developed as part of a team of five developers. Working in a team meant following shared coding standards, reviewing each other's work and maintaining a consistent architecture across both the frontend and backend.

The application includes role-based authentication, user management, Excel import and export, workplace learning evaluations and an administration portal for managing the different users within the platform. A strong emphasis was placed on maintainability by using a vertical slice architecture, CQRS and clear project conventions.

WPL Kompas gave me the opportunity to work on both the frontend and backend while collaborating closely with other developers in a professional workflow. Through this project I gained valuable experience with modern .NET development, React, SQL Server, Docker and team-based software development.
mindmetric-description:
MindMetric is a web application built for sports teams to help coaches keep track of their players' mental well-being. The goal of the platform is to make it easier to spot changes in a player's well-being before they become bigger issues and encourage conversations around mental health within the team.

Players regularly complete short check-ins to share how they are feeling. Coaches can then view the results through a clear dashboard that gives an overview and the progress of players over time. This allows them to identify trends, notice sudden changes and provide support when needed.

One of the main goals during development was to keep the application simple and easy to use. Players should be able to complete a check-in in just a few moments while coaches can quickly understand the collected data without having to search through complicated reports.

MindMetric was developed as a full stack web application with a strong focus on usability, responsive design and maintainable code. The project allowed me to work on both the frontend and backend while creating a solution that addresses a real challenge within sports teams.
stuff-description:
Stuff is a mobile shopping application (made as schooltask) built with React Native and Expo. The app allows users to browse products, manage their favorites and shopping cart and view detailed product information through a clean and responsive interface.

During development, I focused heavily on creating a polished user experience. I designed a custom animated bottom navigation bar, built a dynamic background that adapts to the active theme and created reusable UI components to keep the interface consistent throughout the application. Every screen was designed with smooth interactions and visual consistency in mind.

The application uses React Query to retrieve product data from an external API while Redux Toolkit manages the shopping cart and favorites. Reusable components such as custom loaders, empty states and typography help keep the codebase maintainable and the user experience consistent across the app.

Stuff gave me the opportunity to strengthen my React Native skills while exploring mobile UI design, animations and state management. The project reflects my attention to detail and my interest in creating applications that feel intuitive and enjoyable to use.
